Part: Governance and Responsibility.

Chapter: Constitution and Bylaws Reference.
Current document status: Reference.

Section: Constitution and Bylaws Reference.

This page gives leaders a single place to find the current KBC Constitution, Bylaws, and Covenant reference while reviewing financial operations documents.

Note: Congregation Final Authority.
Use the Constitution and Bylaws to confirm when the congregation must approve budgets, officers, policies, major non-budgeted spending, contracts, role changes, or other church decisions. Committee recommendations should not be treated as final when church approval is required.

Section: Primary Reference.

Open KBC Constitution, Bylaws, and Covenant 2018 PDF{ target="_blank" rel="noopener" }.

Source file:

source-materials/bylaws/Constitution, Bylaws, and Covenant 2018.pdf.

Archived editable conversion:

source-materials/bylaws/archived/KBC_Constitution_ByLaws_Converted to Editable form.docx.

Section: How To Use This Reference.

Treat the PDF as a governance reference, not as a new policy draft.
Confirm with the Church Clerk, Pastor, Deacons, or church leadership that this is the current authoritative version before relying on it for a final decision.
Use this page when reviewing Treasurer duties, committee authority, business meeting votes, staff roles, budget decisions, spending authority, and annual financial review requirements.
Do not treat summaries in this repository as a substitute for the bylaws themselves.

Section: Financial Operations Areas To Check.

These are the main areas in the current project that should be checked against the Constitution and Bylaws:

Table summary.
Area: Church authority and congregational votes. Why It Matters: Some decisions may require church action rather than committee action alone.. Related Working Documents: Responsibility Matrix, Spending Authority Policy.
Area: Treasurer role. Why It Matters: Treasurer duties, election, reporting, bonding, and annual review may be defined or constrained by bylaws.. Related Working Documents: Treasurer Governance Role, Treasurer vs. Bookkeeper Duty Split.
Area: Nonministerial staff. Why It Matters: The Bookkeeper / Financial Administrator role should fit the existing authority for nonministerial staff and the June 28, 2026 congregational motion.. Related Working Documents: Bookkeeper Job Description, Bookkeeper Hiring Process.
Area: Personnel Committee authority. Why It Matters: Personnel Committee responsibilities for employment matters, salaries, benefits, policies, and job descriptions should be confirmed.. Related Working Documents: Personnel Committee Role, Personnel Committee Packet.
Area: Finance / stewardship responsibilities. Why It Matters: Budget preparation, approval, funds handling, reporting, and accounting procedures may affect Finance Committee recommendations.. Related Working Documents: Finance Committee Charter, Monthly Financial Review Policy.
Area: Annual budget and major non-budgeted expenses. Why It Matters: Congregational authority should remain clear where the annual budget, major projects, or large outside-budget spending require a vote.. Related Working Documents: Spending Authority Policy, Responsibility Matrix.
Area: Deacon counsel and escalation. Why It Matters: Deacons may have a meaningful review or counsel role, but final authority and committee lanes should be confirmed before assigning decision power.. Related Working Documents: Church Organization Chart, Future Governance Expansion.
Area: Benevolence and other committee spending. Why It Matters: Confidential care and committee spending may need future review to balance privacy, oversight, reporting, and approval authority.. Related Working Documents: Future Governance Expansion, Open Questions.
Area: Annual audit or review. Why It Matters: Treasurer and Finance Committee responsibilities should align with any bylaw language about annual audit or account review.. Related Working Documents: Audit and Review Policy, Monthly Finance Committee Checklist.

Section: Practical Review Questions.
